questionBank.question: If s(t) is position (meters), what are units of s'(t)?
questionBank.explanation: Derivative of position with respect to time is velocity.
questionBank.notesSnippet: Units follow output/input: meters per second.
questionBank.timeTip: Always track units for context questions.

questionBank.question: For A=pi r^2, if dr/dt=2 and r=3, then dA/dt=
questionBank.notesSnippet: Differentiate equation with respect to time.

questionBank.question: Linearization of f at x=a is:
questionBank.notesSnippet: Use tangent line to approximate nearby values.

questionBank.question: L'Hospital's Rule applies directly to which form?
questionBank.explanation: Rule applies to 0/0 or infinity/infinity quotient forms.
questionBank.notesSnippet: Convert to quotient if needed before using.

frqBank.title: Unit 4 FRQ - Motion and Rates
frqBank.context: A particle moves on a line with position s(t)=t^3-6t^2+9t.

parts.label: (a)
parts.question: Find velocity v(t) and acceleration a(t).
parts.scoring: 1 pt velocity, 1 pt acceleration, 1 pt correct simplification.

parts.label: (b)
parts.question: Find times when particle is at rest.
parts.scoring: 1 pt equation, 1 pt factoring, 1 pt roots.

4.1.title: 4-1 to 4-3 Rates in Context
4.1.content:
Units matter

Always state units for derivatives in applied contexts.

4.2.title: 4-4 Related Rates
4.2.content:
Workflow

Write equation, differentiate with respect to time, then substitute known values.

4.3.title: 4-6 and 4-7 Approximation + L'Hospital
4.3.content:
Approximation tools

Use linearization for nearby estimates and L'Hospital for qualifying indeterminate forms.
